definition: To reach an agreement through formal discussion and compromise.
examples: We spent hours trying to negotiate a better price with the supplier.
She knows how to negotiate a fair salary.

definition: The latest time or date by which something must be completed.
examples: The project deadline is next Friday.
We missed the deadline because of a technical issue.

definition: A list of items to be discussed or done at a meeting.
examples: Let's move on to the next item on the agenda.
Please send me the agenda before the meeting.

definition: A person or group with an interest or concern in a business or project.
examples: We need to keep all stakeholders informed of the changes.
Every stakeholder must approve the new strategy.

definition: To assign a task or responsibility to another person.
examples: A good manager knows how to delegate work effectively.
He delegated the report to his assistant.

definition: The total income generated by a business from its activities.
examples: The company's revenue grew by 12% last year.
Advertising is their main source of revenue.

definition: To integrate a new employee or client into an organization or system.
examples: It takes about two weeks to onboard a new hire.
We onboarded three major clients this quarter.

definition: Happening or produced once every three months.
examples: The board reviews the quarterly earnings report.
We hold a quarterly performance review.

definition: A document listing goods or services provided and the amount owed.
examples: Please pay the invoice within 30 days.
I'll send you the invoice by email tomorrow.

definition: To hire an outside company to perform work instead of doing it internally.
examples: Many firms outsource their customer support overseas.
We decided to outsource the design work.

definition: To use something to maximum advantage or effect.
examples: We can leverage our brand to enter new markets.
The startup leveraged social media to grow quickly.

definition: A standard or point of reference against which things are measured.
examples: This product sets the benchmark for quality in the industry.
We use last year's figures as a benchmark.

definition: To make a process more efficient by simplifying it.
examples: The new software will streamline our workflow.
They streamlined the approval process to save time.

definition: Something that motivates or encourages someone to do something.
examples: The company offers bonuses as an incentive to work harder.
Tax breaks are an incentive for new investment.

definition: A general agreement reached by a group as a whole.
examples: We reached a consensus after a long discussion.
There was no consensus on how to proceed.
